failure_analysis: The diagnostic shows repeated_event_exploitation flag active, but the only event rule (r_contact_one_time) is already one-time, so this is likely a false positive. The dominant reward component is r_distance (ratio 0.437), and the episode reward mean is negative (-32.6), indicating dense penalties are overpowering the landing bonus. This is similar to memory card iter_003_to_004 where a dominant dense penalty (r_velocity) was reduced, leading to improvement.
memory_usage: Memory iter_003_to_004 successfully reduced the weight of a dominant dense penalty (r_velocity) to improve reward balance. Memory iter_001_to_002 showed that clipping the same component (r_distance) led to worse performance, so clipping is avoided. Memory iter_000_to_001’s one-time conversion is already applied to r_contact. Therefore, the most relevant precedent is to decrease the weight of the dominant dense component.
operator_reasoning: decrease_weight on r_distance is valid because r_distance is a distance_penalty component in the schema and exists. Factor 0.5 will halve its contribution, reducing its dominance while still providing shaping signal.
diagnosis: Dense penalty r_distance overshadows other components; reduce its weight to balance rewards and potentially improve learning.
